mpeg_o.protocols
================

.. py:module:: mpeg_o.protocols

.. autoapi-nested-parse::

   Domain protocols defining the five cross-cutting capabilities
   (CV annotation, encryption, indexability, provenance, streamability).

   Each protocol mirrors the corresponding Objective-C
   ``@protocol`` declaration in ``objc/Source/Protocols/``. See
   :doc:`/api-review-v0.6` for the three-language parity table.



Submodules
----------

.. toctree::
   :maxdepth: 1

   /autoapi/mpeg_o/protocols/cv_annotatable/index
   /autoapi/mpeg_o/protocols/encryptable/index
   /autoapi/mpeg_o/protocols/indexable/index
   /autoapi/mpeg_o/protocols/provenanceable/index
   /autoapi/mpeg_o/protocols/streamable/index


Classes
-------

.. autoapisummary::

   mpeg_o.protocols.CVAnnotatable
   mpeg_o.protocols.Encryptable
   mpeg_o.protocols.Indexable
   mpeg_o.protocols.Provenanceable
   mpeg_o.protocols.Streamable


Package Contents
----------------

.. py:class:: CVAnnotatable

   Bases: :py:obj:`Protocol`


   Capability for objects that can be tagged with controlled-vocabulary
   parameters from any ontology (PSI-MS, nmrCV, CHEBI, BFO, ...).

   This is the primary extensibility mechanism in MPEG-O: the schema
   stays minimal while semantic richness lives in curated external
   ontologies.

   .. method:: add_cv_param(param)

      Attach a ``CVParam`` to this object.

   .. method:: remove_cv_param(param)

      Detach a previously-attached ``CVParam``. No-op if absent.

   .. method:: all_cv_params()

      Return every attached ``CVParam`` in insertion order.

   .. method:: cv_params_for_accession(accession)

      Return every attached ``CVParam`` whose ``accession`` matches.

   .. method:: cv_params_for_ontology_ref(ontology_ref)

      Return every attached ``CVParam`` whose ``ontology_ref`` matches.

   .. method:: has_cv_param_with_accession(accession)

      Return ``True`` if at least one attached ``CVParam`` matches.
      

   .. rubric:: Notes

   API status: Stable.

   Cross-language equivalents
   --------------------------
   Objective-C: ``MPGOCVAnnotatable`` ·
   Java: ``com.dtwthalion.mpgo.protocols.CVAnnotatable``


   .. py:method:: add_cv_param(param: mpeg_o.cv_param.CVParam) -> None


   .. py:method:: remove_cv_param(param: mpeg_o.cv_param.CVParam) -> None


   .. py:method:: all_cv_params() -> list[mpeg_o.cv_param.CVParam]


   .. py:method:: cv_params_for_accession(accession: str) -> list[mpeg_o.cv_param.CVParam]


   .. py:method:: cv_params_for_ontology_ref(ontology_ref: str) -> list[mpeg_o.cv_param.CVParam]


   .. py:method:: has_cv_param_with_accession(accession: str) -> bool


.. py:class:: Encryptable

   Bases: :py:obj:`Protocol`


   Capability for MPEG-G-style multi-level content protection.

   Encryption can be applied at dataset-group, dataset,
   descriptor-stream, or access-unit granularity, enabling selective
   protection (for example, encrypting intensity values while leaving
   m/z and scan metadata readable for indexing and search).

   .. method:: encrypt_with_key(key, level)

      Encrypt this object's protectable content at the given
      granularity.

   .. method:: decrypt_with_key(key)

      Decrypt previously-encrypted content.

   .. method:: access_policy()

      Return the current access policy.

   .. method:: set_access_policy(policy)

      Replace the current access policy.
      

   .. rubric:: Notes

   API status: Stable.

   Cross-language equivalents
   --------------------------
   Objective-C: ``MPGOEncryptable`` ·
   Java: ``com.dtwthalion.mpgo.protocols.Encryptable``


   .. py:method:: encrypt_with_key(key: bytes, level: mpeg_o.enums.EncryptionLevel) -> None


   .. py:method:: decrypt_with_key(key: bytes) -> None


   .. py:method:: access_policy() -> object | None


   .. py:method:: set_access_policy(policy: object | None) -> None


.. py:class:: Indexable

   Bases: :py:obj:`Protocol`


   Capability for collections that support O(1) random access.

   Every conformer provides index-based access. Key-based and
   range-based access are optional; conformers that do not support
   them raise NotImplementedError.

   .. method:: object_at_index(index)

      Return the element at index (0-based).

   .. method:: count()

      Return the total number of elements.

   .. method:: object_for_key(key)

      Optional. Return the element associated with key or raise
      KeyError.

   .. method:: objects_in_range(start, stop)

      Optional. Return the half-open slice [start, stop).
      

   .. rubric:: Notes

   API status: Stable.

   Cross-language equivalents
   --------------------------
   Objective-C: MPGOIndexable ·
   Java: com.dtwthalion.mpgo.protocols.Indexable


   .. py:method:: object_at_index(index: int) -> Any


   .. py:method:: count() -> int


   .. py:method:: object_for_key(key: Any) -> Any


   .. py:method:: objects_in_range(start: int, stop: int) -> list[Any]


.. py:class:: Provenanceable

   Bases: :py:obj:`Protocol`


   Capability for objects that carry a W3C PROV-compatible chain
   of processing records.

   Every transformation applied to the data contributes an entry;
   the chain makes the object self-documenting and supports
   regulatory audit trails.

   .. method:: add_processing_step(step)

      Append a ``ProvenanceRecord`` to the chain.

   .. method:: provenance_chain()

      Return the full chain in insertion order.

   .. method:: input_entities()

      Return the distinct input entity identifiers referenced by the
      chain.

   .. method:: output_entities()

      Return the distinct output entity identifiers referenced by
      the chain.
      

   .. rubric:: Notes

   API status: Stable.

   Cross-language equivalents
   --------------------------
   Objective-C: ``MPGOProvenanceable`` ·
   Java: ``com.dtwthalion.mpgo.protocols.Provenanceable``


   .. py:method:: add_processing_step(step: mpeg_o.provenance.ProvenanceRecord) -> None


   .. py:method:: provenance_chain() -> list[mpeg_o.provenance.ProvenanceRecord]


   .. py:method:: input_entities() -> list[str]


   .. py:method:: output_entities() -> list[str]


.. py:class:: Streamable

   Bases: :py:obj:`Protocol`


   Capability for sequential access with explicit positioning.

   Enables efficient iteration over large datasets without
   materializing the entire collection in memory.

   .. method:: next_object()

      Return the next element and advance the cursor.

   .. method:: has_more()

      Return ``True`` if ``next_object`` can be called.

   .. method:: current_position()

      Return the 0-based position of the next element to be yielded.

   .. method:: seek_to_position(position)

      Reposition the cursor. Returns ``True`` on success.

   .. method:: reset()

      Reposition the cursor to 0.
      

   .. rubric:: Notes

   API status: Stable.

   Cross-language equivalents
   --------------------------
   Objective-C: ``MPGOStreamable`` ·
   Java: ``com.dtwthalion.mpgo.protocols.Streamable``


   .. py:method:: next_object() -> Any


   .. py:method:: has_more() -> bool


   .. py:method:: current_position() -> int


   .. py:method:: seek_to_position(position: int) -> bool


   .. py:method:: reset() -> None
